subparts.text:
What is the purpose of the
tag in HTML? How does it differ from the

tag?
Explain the role of the alt attribute in the tag. Why is it important for web accessibility?
What is the difference between an internal and an external CSS? Provide an example of how external CSS is linked to an HTML document.
What are DTDs in XML? How are they used to define the structure of an XML document?
What is the difference between let, var, and const in JavaScript? Provide a brief example for each.
What is the main advantage of using AJAX in web applications? How does it improve user experience?
Explain the role of a CMS. How does it simplify website development?
What is the purpose of mysqli_connect in PHP? Write a simple example of establishing a connection to a MySQL database.

questions.question_text: Design a simple HTML page structure that includes:  A heading.  A paragraph with emphasized text.  A horizontal rule.  An ordered list with three items and an unordered list with two items.  A table with two rows and three columns, including appropriate headings. Provide the HTML code and explain the functionality of each tag used.

Create an HTML form for user registration that includes:  A textbox for the user's name.  A password field.  A dropdown list for gender selection.  A multi-line text area for the address.  A submit button. Write the HTML code and explain how each form element works. 2

Explain the key components of XML and how they are used to structure data. Create an example XML document for a bookstore that includes details such as book titles, authors, prices, and publication dates.

Write a CSS and DHTML-based example to create a web page where:  CSS is used for styling the page with a background color, font styles, and positioning elements.  A DHTML feature is implemented to dynamically change the color of a paragraph when the user clicks a button. Provide the HTML, CSS, and JavaScript code along with an explanation of the implementation.

Write a JavaScript program that includes:  A function to validate a form input .  Event handling to display a success message if the validation passes. Provide the code and explain the role of each component, including form properties, conditions, and event handling.

Explain how AJAX works in web applications, and demonstrate with an example where:  A button click fetches data from a server without reloading the page.  Use JavaScript and XMLHttpRequest to implement the functionality. Provide the code and explain the flow of the AJAX request and response.

Write a PHP script that:  Connects to a MySQL database.  Creates a table named Users with fields for ID, Name, and Email.  Inserts three sample records into the table.  Displays all the records in an HTML table format. Provide the complete code and explain each part.

Compare the WordPress and Drupal as content management systems based on the following:  Discuss their benefits, use cases, and key differences in terms of theme customization, plugin/module development, and scalability.  Provide examples of scenarios where one would be more suitable than the other.
